question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: cacheManagerSystemFileExchangeRates()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a contract call immediately after an exchange rate update so that the stale value in the `exchangeRate` Caffeine cache (backed by `cacheProperties.getFee()`) is used to compute HBAR-to-USD conversion, causing the user to pay fewer fees than required and draining protocol revenue?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: cacheManagerSystemFileExchangeRates()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a hard-coded TTL on the `CACHE_MANAGER_EXCHANGE_RATES_SYSTEM_FILE` bean (TTL is delegated entirely to `cacheProperties.getFee()`) to cause the exchange rate cache to serve an arbitrarily old rate, allowing the user to execute token purchases at a stale favorable rate and extract value from other users?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: cacheManagerToken()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared `CACHE_MANAGER_TOKEN` cache manager—which stores `CACHE_NAME_TOKEN_ALLOWANCE`, `CACHE_NAME_NFT_ALLOWANCE`, `CACHE_NAME_TOKEN`, `CACHE_NAME_TOKEN_ACCOUNT`, `CACHE_NAME_TOKEN_ACCOUNT_COUNT`, `CACHE_NAME_TOKEN_AIRDROP` all under the same Caffeine spec—to read a stale token allowance that was already revoked on-chain, and use that stale allowance to authorize a token transfer, causing direct loss of funds to the token owner?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: cacheManagerToken()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a sequence of EVM calls where a token allowance is revoked between two calls, but the `CACHE_NAME_TOKEN_ALLOWANCE` entry in `cacheManagerToken()` has not yet expired, causing the second call to see the old allowance as still valid and execute an unauthorized transfer?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: cacheManagerToken()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `CACHE_NAME_NFT_ALLOWANCE` and `CACHE_NAME_TOKEN_ALLOWANCE` share the same Caffeine cache specification (via `cacheProperties.getToken()`) to cause a cache size eviction that removes a valid allowance entry, then re-query it and receive a freshly loaded but incorrect (e.g., revoked) allowance state, leading to unauthorized NFT transfers?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: cacheManagerRecordFileLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `cacheManagerRecordFileLatest()` bean's `maximumSize(1)` and `expireAfterWrite(500, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS)` configuration to race between the 500ms window and submit a contract call that reads the latest block state before a critical balance-changing transaction is reflected, causing the EVM to execute against a stale world state and produce incorrect fund transfers?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: cacheManagerRecordFileLatest()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a high volume of concurrent `eth_call` requests within the 500ms TTL window of `cacheManagerRecordFileLatest()` to force all calls to execute against the same stale latest-block snapshot, causing balance checks to reflect pre-transfer state and enabling double-spend-like reads?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: cacheManagerRecordFileEarliest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `cacheManagerRecordFileEarliest()` bean which has NO expiry policy (`Caffeine.newBuilder().maximumSize(1)`) to permanently lock the earliest record file reference to the genesis block, causing historical EVM calls that depend on the earliest block to always execute against genesis state and misreport balances or allowances?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: cacheManagerRecordFileEarliest()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where the `cacheManagerRecordFileEarliest()` cache (no TTL, max size 1) permanently caches an incorrect earliest block reference, causing all subsequent historical queries to use a wrong baseline block and return incorrect token balances or allowances?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: cacheManagerRecordFileIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `cacheManagerRecordFileIndex()` bean's 10-minute `expireAfterWrite` TTL and `maximumSize(10000)` to cause a historical contract call to resolve a block index to a wrong record file, leading the EVM to execute against an incorrect historical state and return wrong token balances?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: cacheManagerRecordFileIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the `cacheManagerRecordFileIndex()` cache (max 10000 entries) with 10000 distinct block index lookups to evict legitimate entries, causing subsequent historical EVM calls to re-fetch block indices from the database and potentially race with concurrent writes, leading to incorrect state reads?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: cacheManagerRecordFileTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `cacheManagerRecordFileTimestamp()` bean's `expireAfterAccess(10, TimeUnit.MINUTES)` policy—which resets TTL on every access—to keep a stale timestamp-to-record-file mapping alive indefinitely by repeatedly accessing it, causing historical EVM calls to always resolve to the same stale block and misreport balances?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: cacheManagerRecordFileTimestamp()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the difference between `expireAfterWrite` (used in `cacheManagerRecordFileIndex`) and `expireAfterAccess` (used in `cacheManagerRecordFileTimestamp`) to keep a timestamp cache entry alive past the point where the underlying record file has been updated, causing EVM historical calls to use wrong block data and return incorrect fund balances?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: provideGasCalculator()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a miscalculation in `CustomGasCalculator` (returned by `provideGasCalculator()`) that undercharges gas for specific EVM opcodes, allowing the user to execute computationally expensive operations at below-cost gas prices, draining node resources and indirectly causing economic loss to the network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: provideGasCalculator()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a contract call that exploits `CustomGasCalculator` returning incorrect intrinsic gas costs for specific call data patterns (e.g., zero-byte vs non-zero-byte miscounting), causing the EVM to undercharge gas and allow execution of calls that should be rejected as exceeding the gas limit?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: provideGasCalculator()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit `CustomGasCalculator` (singleton bean from `provideGasCalculator()`) if it contains mutable state that is not thread-safe, causing concurrent contract calls to corrupt each other's gas accounting and allow one call to execute with another call's gas budget, leading to fund loss?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: entityIdFactory()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `entityIdFactory()` bean which is initialized with `evmProperties.getVersionedConfiguration()` at startup—a lazily computed value—to cause entity ID resolution to use a configuration snapshot that does not reflect the current chain ID or shard/realm, leading to cross-shard entity ID collisions and incorrect fund routing?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: entityIdFactory()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a contract call that resolves an EVM address to a Hedera entity ID via `AppEntityIdFactory` using a stale `VersionedConfiguration` (captured at bean initialization), causing the address-to-account mapping to be wrong and funds to be sent to the wrong account?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: cacheManagerContractState()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `cacheManagerContractState()` bean's cache (backed by `cacheProperties.getContractState()`) to read a stale contract storage slot value after a state-changing transaction, causing the EVM to execute a contract call with incorrect storage state and produce wrong token transfer amounts?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: cacheManagerContractState()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `CACHE_MANAGER_CONTRACT_STATE` cache to read a contract's storage slot that was updated by a recent transaction but not yet evicted from cache, causing the EVM to use the pre-update slot value in a balance calculation and return incorrect results?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: cacheManagerContractSlots()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `cacheManagerContractSlots()` bean (using `CACHE_NAME = \

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: cacheManagerSlotsPerContract()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `cacheManagerSlotsPerContract()` bean (which does NOT call `setCacheNames()`, leaving dynamic cache creation enabled) to cause unbounded cache growth by querying arbitrary contract addresses, exhausting JVM heap memory and causing a denial of service that prevents legitimate fund transfers?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: cacheManagerSlotsPerContract()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `cacheManagerSlotsPerContract()` does not call `setCacheNames()` (unlike other cache managers), allowing Caffeine to dynamically create new caches for any cache name requested, to inject a cache name collision that overwrites a legitimate cache entry and causes wrong slot counts to be used in EVM execution?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: cacheManagerEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `cacheManagerEntity()` bean which registers `CACHE_NAME`, `CACHE_NAME_EVM_ADDRESS`, and `CACHE_NAME_ALIAS` under the same Caffeine spec to cause a stale EVM address-to-entity mapping to persist, allowing the user to resolve a recently-deleted account's EVM address to the old entity and redirect funds to it?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/config/EvmConfiguration.java -> Scope: Critical: Direct loss of funds'] [Function: cacheManagerEntity()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `CACHE_NAME_ALIAS` cache within `cacheManagerEntity()` to read a stale alias-to-entity mapping after an alias has been reassigned to a different account, causing fund transfers targeting that alias to be routed to the old account?
